channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: IOWN Global Forum targets datacenter interconnects to scatter AI infrastructure
item.description: The IOWN Global Forum is set to prioritize datacenter interconnects to enhance AI infrastructure, aiming to support diverse providers in the industry.

item.title: Cisco Wi-Fi boxes are filling their disks with 5MB of undeletable data every day
item.description: A critical flaw in over 230 Cisco Wi-Fi access points causes them to generate 5MB of undeletable data daily, potentially hindering necessary software updates.

item.title: IPv6 carried half of internet traffic – for one day, according to Google
item.description: Google reported that IPv6 accounted for half of internet traffic for just one day in March, highlighting ongoing challenges in widespread adoption.

item.title: I traded my Sonos Era 300 for Denon's new home speaker - and see no reason to go back
item.description: The Denon Home 400 speaker has been reviewed as a strong competitor to Sonos' Era 300, featuring improved hardware and software.

item.title: AI-powered website builders have come a long way - here's your best option in 2026
item.description: AI website builders have significantly improved, with some now capable of delivering high-quality websites, marking a shift from their previous limitations.

item.title: Amazon just slashed $250 off the Google Pixel 10 - and a Prime subscription isn't required
item.description: Amazon has reduced the price of the Google Pixel 10 by $250, making it more accessible while maintaining flagship features.

item.title: I found the apps slowing down my PC - how to kill the biggest memory hogs
item.description: A guide reveals how to identify and eliminate memory-hogging applications that slow down PCs, enhancing performance.

item.title: These companies are actually upskilling their workers for AI - here's how they do it
item.description: Companies are actively upskilling their workforce for AI roles, focusing on entry-level positions and effective training strategies.

item.title: Get a free pair of Meta Ray-Bans with this Verizon Fios Home internet deal - how to redeem it
item.description: Verizon Fios is offering a free pair of Meta Ray-Ban smart glasses with certain home internet plans, promoting their services.

item.title: Anthropic won't own MCP 'design flaw' putting 200K servers at risk, researchers say
item.description: A design flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol (MCP) could jeopardize 200,000 servers, raising security concerns among researchers.

item.title: Visual Studio 18.5 lands with AI debugging at a price, devs still feeling blue
item.description: Microsoft's Visual Studio 18.5 introduces AI debugging and smarter code suggestions, but ongoing developer frustrations over color contrast and updates remain significant issues.

item.title: Meta raises Quest 3 and Quest 3S prices due to RAM shortage
item.description: Meta is increasing prices for the Quest 3 and Quest 3S due to a RAM shortage, with price hikes effective from April 19.

item.title: Google’s AI Mode update lets you open links without leaving the page
item.description: Google's Chrome is enhancing its AI Mode, allowing users to open links side by side with chat, improving multitasking and information access.

item.title: Canva’s AI assistant can now call various tools to make designs for you
item.description: Canva's AI assistant has been updated to create editable designs using text prompts, streamlining the design process for users.

item.title: Making AI operational in constrained public sector environments
item.description: Public sector organizations are under pressure to adopt AI, facing unique challenges that small language models could help address effectively.

item.title: This simulation startup wants to be the Cursor for physical AI
item.description: Antioch has raised $8.5 million to develop simulation tools aimed at supporting the next generation of robotics builders.

item.title: The Download: cyberscammers’ banking bypasses, and carbon removal troubles
item.description: The Download newsletter discusses how cyberscammers are using illicit tools to bypass bank security, highlighting ongoing security challenges.

item.title: SoundHound AI Stock Soars 12% on Short Squeeze Hopes: Buy the Voice AI Momentum or Sell?
item.description: SoundHound AI's stock surged over 12% amid speculation of a short squeeze ahead of its upcoming earnings report, igniting trading interest.

item.title: graphifyy 0.4.18
item.description: The graphifyy 0.4.18 update introduces an AI coding assistant that creates queryable knowledge graphs from various types of files.

item.title: feishu-bridge 2026.4.16.4
item.description: The feishu-bridge update enables remote AI agent control with over 80 API commands, enhancing integration capabilities.

item.title: voxpact added to PyPI
item.description: VoxPact has launched an official Python SDK, enabling developers to create applications for its AI-to-AI agent marketplace. This addition enhances accessibility for developers in the AI ecosystem.

item.title: Microsoft Fixes Windows Server 2019 and 2022 Bug That Caused Unexpected Upgrades to Server 2025
item.description: Microsoft has resolved a significant bug that caused Windows Server 2019 and 2022 to upgrade to Windows Server 2025 without user consent. This fix is crucial for maintaining user control over system updates.

item.title: Deputy minister urges on-target strategy to close AI development gap
item.description: Stella Christie, Deputy Minister of Higher Education, emphasizes the need for a focused strategy to bridge the AI development gap in the country. This call highlights the importance of targeted investments in AI education and research.

item.title: sf-behaviour 1.0.1
item.description: The sf-behaviour 1.0.1 has been released, providing a behavior test runner for OpenAI-compatible endpoints, built on the spanforge framework. This tool aids developers in testing AI models effectively.

item.title: sirius-chat 0.27.11
item.description: The sirius-chat 0.27.11 framework has been launched, enabling multi-user RPG dialogue using OpenAI providers. This framework allows for more interactive and engaging AI conversations in gaming.

item.title: Google Launches Native Gemini App for macOS With Keyboard Shortcut Access and Screen Sharing
item.description: Google has introduced a native Gemini app for macOS, featuring keyboard shortcuts and screen sharing capabilities. This launch enhances user experience for macOS users engaging with Gemini.

item.title: Intel To Disclose Full Details of Elon’s Ground-Breaking TeraFab Project That Will Reshape The Chipmaking Industry
item.description: Intel is set to reveal comprehensive details about Elon Musk's TeraFab project, which aims to revolutionize the chipmaking industry. This announcement is highly anticipated and could significantly impact semiconductor manufacturing.

item.title: graphifyy 0.4.17
item.description: The graphifyy 0.4.17 coding assistant skill has been released, allowing users to create queryable knowledge graphs from various coding resources. This tool aids in organizing and accessing coding information efficiently.

item.title: Chatbots at the ballot box: AI skirts Brazil election rules
item.description: Brazil's electoral court has banned AI chatbots from providing voting tips for the upcoming presidential election to combat disinformation. Despite this, tests show that popular chatbots still rank candidates, raising concerns about compliance.

item.title: Foldable iPhone vs Galaxy Z Fold 2026: Which Premium Book-Style Device Should You Buy?
item.description: As the foldable smartphone market heats up, consumers are comparing Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold 8 with Apple's anticipated foldable iPhone, both expected to launch in 2026. This competition could reshape the premium smartphone landscape.

item.title: Samsung Raises US Prices on Galaxy Z Flip 7, S25 FE, Tablets, and More
item.description: Samsung has raised prices on several of its smartphones and tablets in the U.S., including the Galaxy Z Flip 7 and S25 FE. This move reflects the company's response to market conditions and inflationary pressures.

item.title: How does OpenAI’s Agents SDK sandboxing work? #tech
item.description: OpenAI has updated its Agents SDK to enhance security and operational reliability for AI systems, introducing native sandboxing for safer in-house agent testing. This update aims to improve the safety of AI interactions.

item.title: What did Anthropic’s Claude identity verification add? #tech
item.description: Anthropic has implemented a new identity verification step for its Claude AI, requiring users to provide a government-issued photo ID for access to certain capabilities. This aims to enhance security and trust in AI interactions.

item.title: graphifyy 0.4.16
item.description: The latest version of graphifyy allows users to convert folders of code, documents, and media into a queryable knowledge graph, enhancing accessibility and organization of information. This tool is aimed at improving AI coding assistance.

item.title: Command integrity breaks in the LLM routing layer
item.description: A report discusses vulnerabilities in the LLM routing layer, which intermediates requests to AI models. Issues in this layer can affect the integrity and reliability of AI outputs.

item.title: Experts Warn of Widespread Job Losses and Global Power Struggles Amid AI Advancements
item.description: Experts warn that advancements in AI could lead to significant job losses and global power shifts, with Elon Musk emphasizing the urgent need for regulation. The report underscores AI's potential to disrupt economies and societies.

item.title: Report Alleges Iran Used Chinese Satellite for Surveillance Prior to Strikes on U.S. Bases
item.description: A leaked report claims Iran's military used a Chinese satellite for surveillance of U.S. bases prior to military strikes. This raises concerns about international security and the implications of surveillance technologies.

item.title: TSMC’s Profit Surges 58% On AI-Driven Chip Demand
item.description: TSMC reported a 58% increase in profits, driven by strong demand for AI-driven chips. This indicates resilience in the semiconductor industry despite geopolitical tensions.

item.title: OneQode, Hitachi Vantara & Cylix Form Strategic Alliance to Launch Sovereign AI Factory Initiative
item.description: OneQode, Hitachi Vantara, and Cylix have formed a strategic alliance to launch the Sovereign AI Factory Initiative, aiming to enhance AI capabilities in data management and infrastructure. This collaboration seeks to address growing demands for sovereign data solutions.

item.title: China’s Parallel Web Behind the Wall | Vale.Rocks
item.description: The article discusses China's heavily regulated internet, contrasting it with the global web. It highlights the censorship and control mechanisms that shape online experiences within China.

item.title: AgentSeal/codeburn: See where your AI coding tokens go. Interactive TUI dashboard for Claude Code and Codex cost observability.
item.description: AgentSeal has launched an interactive dashboard for tracking AI coding token usage for Claude Code and Codex, enhancing cost observability for developers.

item.title: Snap CEO Evan Spiegel calls it a “crucible moment” after announcing 1,000 job cuts
item.description: Snap Inc. announced it will cut 1,000 jobs, about 16% of its workforce, to streamline operations and focus on sustainable growth amid financial challenges.

item.title: deepsteer added to PyPI
item.description: Deepsteer has been added to PyPI, focusing on evaluating and steering alignment depth during the pre-training of large language models.

item.title: Jensen Huang explains why Nvidia invests in tons of companies, instead of trying to pick winners
item.description: Nvidia's CEO Jensen Huang explained the company's strategy of investing broadly in various companies instead of selecting specific winners, citing historical lessons.

item.title: Datavault AI 於紐約和費城啟用首批邊緣 GPU 據點；價值 14.4 億至 19.2 億美元的量子就緒機隊，預計將於 2026 年底前覆蓋全美 100 多個城市
item.description: Datavault AI is launching a fleet of 48,000 quantum-ready GPUs across the U.S. by 2026, targeting enterprises with long GPU lead times.

item.title: torch-aac 0.5.0
item.description: Torch-aac 0.5.0 has been released, featuring a GPU-accelerated and differentiable AAC-LC encoder built with PyTorch.

item.title: reviewd 0.6.3
item.description: Reviewd 0.6.3 is a local AI code reviewer for GitHub and BitBucket, utilizing Claude, Gemini, or Codex to review pull requests.

item.title: How the AI boom is reshaping tech cost management
item.description: FinOps professionals are increasingly managing AI costs, optimizing token usage, and aligning cost-saving measures with sustainability goals.

item.title: Musk's impending SpaceX financial revolution, deceiving the world through propaganda
item.description: Elon Musk is reportedly preparing for a significant financial move with SpaceX, raising questions about transparency and trust in his ventures.

item.title: Terafab Equipment and Chip Orders
item.description: Elon Musk has placed orders for equipment to produce 3,000 wafers monthly for the Terafab, indicating a significant scale-up in chip production.
